name: Chewy and Crusty Thick Pizza Dough
recipeIngredient: 1 pound starchy potatoes, peeled and cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the potatoes in the boiling water for 20 minutes or until very tender. Drain, reserving 1 cup of cooking liquid.
2. Let the cooking liquid cool to a warm temperature (120 to 130°F). Mash the potatoes and set aside.
3. In a large bowl, combine 1/2 cup flour and yeast. Stir well to combine.
4. Add the mashed potato-yeast mixture and oil to the yeast mixture. Stir until combined. Let stand for 10 minutes.
5. Pulse 3 1/2 cups flour and salt in a food processor or mix in a bowl with a dough hook until just combined.
6. With the processor running, add the potato-yeast mixture and oil through the feed tube. Process until the dough forms a ball that leaves the sides of the bowl.
7. Transfer the dough to a lightly sprayed large bowl. Turn to coat the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F), free from drafts, for 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
8. Punch down the dough and turn out onto a floured surface. Knead 2 or 3 times. Divide into 2 portions and form each into a ball. Set aside covered for 15 minutes.
9. Pat each ball into a 12-14 inch circle on a cornmeal-sprinkled baking sheet. Bake at 500°F for 8-10 minutes.
